message:
fix(fr-runtime): tag ACL log context as "lua" during script execution

current_acl_log_context() previously returned "multi" only when the session was
inside MULTI/EXEC and otherwise fell through to the default ("toplevel" via the
downstream map). When an ACL-protected command is rejected from inside a Lua
script (EVAL/EVALSHA/FUNCTION), Redis reports the log context as "lua" in
ACL LOG and related INFO fields. Our runtime was missing that branch, so
script-originated ACL failures appeared as plain toplevel entries and diverged
from Redis observability.

Hoist the Lua detection ahead of the MULTI/EXEC branch by consulting
`self.server.store.script_nesting_level > 0` — the store-level reentrancy
counter that already tracks active EVAL/FUNCTION frames — and return "lua"
first; MULTI/EXEC detection stays in place as the secondary branch.

NOT committing:
- .beads/issues.jsonl: local flush would revert frankenredis-7q4b from closed
  back to open (upstream closed it at 17:05:34 UTC); leaving the beads DB to
  reconcile on the next `br sync --flush-only` from a freshly-synced DB.
- Untracked .beads/*.bad_*/.pre_rebuild_*/recovery_*/ artifacts: ephemeral
  repair/recovery snapshots, not source of truth.
- Root-level `debug`, `debug.rs`, `debug2.rs` and
  `crates/fr-conformance/dump.rdb`: agent scratch / persisted RDB, not
  intended for version control.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
